scope: TileLang query-major selected-K-chunk compact sparse-dKV producer
question: Can a chunk-local active-group accumulator shrink the producer LDS/control wall while preserving the D64 MFMA contribution body?

implementation.read: One CTA owns one token tile, one selected K64 chunk inside a route panel, and one D64 block. It uses the same TileLang BF16 MFMA contribution body as mfma_reduced, accumulates into a chunk-local active compressed-group table, writes compact chunk rows, then folds those rows with torch.index_add_ for this gate.

correctness.read: The small correctness gate passes against the PyTorch final-dKV reference.

route_read.read: The assumed LDS shrink does not materialize at token_block=256. Even one K64 chunk sees almost every group in the 512-group panel across 256 tokens, so the chunk-local table still reaches max_active_groups=512 while multiplying route chunks and compact partial rows.

decision.retain: mfma_reduced tb256/gps512/cap320/k64d64/threads512 with TileLang panel-dense final fold
decision.why: It loses Q/dO reuse and increases compact partial traffic without reducing the live active-group table at the target token tile.
decision.next_work: A useful rewrite must avoid both failure modes: keep panel-level Q/dO reuse and avoid a full 512xD FP32 shared accumulator. Candidate directions are wave/register-local pre-aggregation before touching group_acc, a lower-level HIP/rocWMMA producer with explicit segmented epilogue control, or fusing downstream compressor/LI dKV consumption so the producer does not need to materialize dense compact rows.
